contract.forward_sidecar: safe_groups/group_ids[B,S,K] is the exact forward-known selected compressed-group id for every selected slot.
contract.goal: Keep the fast Triton query-major producer while using exact group ids to reduce dKV contributions before global memory.
contract.not_the_goal: A bounded hottest-group sidecar is not the intended design; it was only a proxy and is not retained as the candidate.

read: Triton already receives the exact forward-known group id sidecar as safe_groups/group_ids; dot_k_atomic uses it directly but performs one global atomic per selected contribution.

The exact-sidecar active-group reducer, dot_k_micro, is not the right schedule: it reduces by exact compressed group but introduces active-group blocks and route-matrix matching, which replays q/dO and pushes S4096 latency to roughly 57-61 ms.

The compact-micro exact-sidecar path preserves more of the fast producer and shows a 7.05 ms no-dKV/diagnostic floor, but its valid partial-store path still writes one selected contribution before final reduction and lands at 8.82 ms, slower than direct atomics.

The HIP full-window exact-sidecar resident valid-dKV path is also rejected at 42.12 ms because the full selected-window page and token_block=1 provide no route write reduction and overload the producer.

The retained design is exact group-id sidecar plus query-major producer, but the reducer must be a narrow in-epilogue segmented accumulator or lower-level cooperative producer-consumer, not hottest-group filtering, full-window residency, or active-group-owner replay.

next_kernel_contract.producer: Triton/HIP query-major H64/N64/D-block style, with q/dO loaded once for the selected-window work it consumes.
next_kernel_contract.sidecar: Exact group_ids[B,S,K] from forward, optionally prepacked as local selected-slot-to-group-slot metadata for the tile.
next_kernel_contract.epilogue: Use exact group ids to perform a narrow segmented reduction before global writes; issue final global atomics only after local reduction.
avoid: HBM partial slabs that still write one row per selected contribution
